summary: Code Is Cheap Now, And That Changes Everything (9 minute read) The rise of AI coding agents has turned software implementation into a low-cost commodity, shifting the primary value of a developer from writing code to defining and verifying complex systems.

title: Fewer Computers, Fewer Problems: Going Local With Builds & Deployments
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.jim-nielsen.com%2F2026%2Ffewer-computers-fewer-problems%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d7717c80b-5a87ce0b-891b-471e-87d0-9c2f55354c84-000000/eo6tbfolE1bO74ztAbepP4uc5a-Kie9LrW8UC-B9PGs=452
summary: Fewer Computers, Fewer Problems: Going Local With Builds & Deployments (4 minute read) Transitioning to a local-first deployment workflow eliminates the complexity of distributed computing for personal projects by making sure that successful local builds translate directly to production without the need to troubleshoot remote infrastructure failures.

title: Introducing Bun as a Runtime for Pulumi
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.pulumi.com%2Fblog%2Fintroducing-bun-as-a-runtime-for-pulumi%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d7727cfb7-98c15ebb-5c93-4217-b8cf-2b48fe4e8b5f-000000/5xJgA91IupPiIGVc2OpaXkqonTB197yfVNO6Bu8mzQs=452
summary: Introducing Bun as a Runtime for Pulumi (2 minute read) Pulumi now supports Bun as a fully supported runtime (not just a package manager) as of version 3.227.0, allowing developers to execute entire Pulumi programs by setting `runtime: bun` in their Pulumi.yaml with no Node.js required. The integration offers faster package installation and native TypeScript support, though it currently doesn't support policy packs, function serialization, or multi-language components that some projects may s...

title: Understanding Traceroute
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftech.stonecharioteer.com%2Fposts%2F2026%2Ftraceroute%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d7727cfb7-98c15ebb-5c93-4217-b8cf-2b48fe4e8b5f-000000/9aX_6cuZFH0JffwltVDizsFuSVRdpCFI6zt24fBOfLQ=452
summary: Understanding Traceroute (16 minute read) Traceroute works by sending packets with increasing TTL values so each router drops them in sequence and returns an ICMP “Time Exceeded” message, revealing each hop's IP until the destination responds with a different ICMP message. By repeating this process with multiple probes and measuring response times, it maps the path and latency of network traffic, though gaps occur when routers don't send replies.

title: Archon
summary: Archon (GitHub Repo) Archon is the first open-source workflow engine for AI coding agents. It allows developers to define development processes like planning, testing, and PR creation as deterministic YAML workflows rather than relying on unpredictable AI behavior. The tool, which works with platforms like Claude Code, includes 17 default workflows, a web dashboard for monitoring, and aims to bring the same standardization to AI coding that Dockerfiles brought to infrastructure.
